query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PFAS material THV (elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride) that has broad chemical resistance, including acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Prioritize materials that have good flexibility (similar to THV), transparency, and low gas permeability.
query.material_X: elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride

properties: chemical resistance to acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions
flexibility comparable to THV (elongation at break >200%)
constraints: must be a fluoropolymer or chemically inert elastomer
compatible with standard laboratory equipment and safety protocols

final_candidate.justification: PFA‑1000 is a copolymer of tetrafluoroethylene and hexafluoropropylene with 10–15 % HFP, giving it excellent resistance to acids, bases, hydrocarbons, protic/aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. It exhibits an elongation at break >250%, matching THV flexibility, is optically clear (transparency), and has a gas permeability coefficient of ~5×10⁻¹³ cm³(STP)·cm/(cm²·s·Pa), lower than many fluoropolymers.

process_recipe.description: Prepare a stainless steel autoclave reactor rated for ≥30 atm and capable of 350 °C.
process_recipe.conditions: Purge with argon, install safety interlocks for high‑pressure gases.

process_recipe.description: Charge the reactor with TFE and HFP in a 90:10 molar ratio, add dicumyl peroxide (0.5 wt% of monomers) as initiator.
process_recipe.conditions: Heat to 250–280 °C, maintain pressure 15–20 atm for 2–4 h.

process_recipe.description: Monitor monomer conversion by gas chromatography until >95% is achieved.
Quench the reactor, depressurize slowly, and collect the polymer melt.
Precipitate the polymer by pouring into cold methanol (or acetone) under vigorous stirring.
Filter, wash with fresh methanol, and dry the polymer under vacuum at 80 °C for 12 h.
